Grand/1742457061439-Welcomheritage 2.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Srinagar/WelcomHeritage Grand/1742457061561-Welcomheritage 4.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Srinagar Local Sightseeing","day_summary":"

After breakfast, begin your exploration of Srinagar with a visit to the revered Shankaracharya Temple, offering panoramic views of the city and Dal Lake. Explore the iconic Mughal Gardens like Shalimar Bagh, Nishat Bagh, and Chashma Shahi, known for their grandeur and tranquility. Continue your tour with visits to Pari Mahal, Botanical Garden, and Nehru Park. Enjoy a relaxing Shikara ride on Dal Lake, followed by a peaceful evening walk along Boulevard Road. End your day with an overnight stay in Srinagar.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Srinagar to Sonamarg","day_summary":"

After breakfast, embark on a scenic drive from Srinagar to Sonamarg, traveling through the picturesque towns of Ganderbal and Kangan. Sonamarg, meaning \"Meadow of Gold,\" offers stunning views of snowy mountains and the sparkling Sindh River. Enjoy a pony ride to Thajiwas Glacier, and try sledge riding on the snow. On your way back, visit the Kheer Bhawani Temple in Tulmulla. Return to Srinagar for an overnight stay.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Srinagar to Gulmarg","day_summary":"

After breakfast, embark to Gulmarg, a stunning hill resort known for its natural beauty. Spend the day at leisure, exploring the lush meadows or enjoying horse riding amidst the picturesque surroundings. Don’t miss the thrilling Gondola ride to Khilanmarg, where you’ll enjoy panoramic views of the snow-capped mountains. In the evening, return to Srinagar for an overnight stay.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Departure Day","day_summary":"

After breakfast, check out from your hotel and head to Srinagar Airport for your onward journey and take a moment to reflect on the beautiful experiences and unforgettable memories created during your stay in Kashmir. Depart with the warmth of Kashmir’s hospitality and scenic beauty.

packages.overview:
Arunachal Pradesh the land of the rising sun and treasure house of nature tucked away in the northeastern tip of India. 10 Days Tawang Tour With Kaziranga shall cover the natural paradise and unexplored land of the northeast. Witness the green tea gardens of Assam with the exotic wildlife of Kaziranga National Park. Get amazed with the changing color of the valley as you enter Arunachal and move ahead toward Tawang. See the beauty of nature at its best and carry amazing memories with insta perfect pictures along.

  • Visit the Famous Tawang Monastery which is one of the oldest in the world and Largest in India.
  • Get amazed with the Amazing & Dense Virgin Forest and Paddy Fields on the Road from Kaziranga.
  • Sight the world famous one-horned rhinoceroses and other wild animals in the World Heritage Sight Kaziranga.
  • Explore the Land of Rising Sun Blessed With beautiful mountains, cascading waterfalls, dense virgin forest etc..
  • Visit the Madhuri lake named after a popular Bollywood Actress - Madhuri Dixit.

Enjoy a comfortable overnight stay at your hotel in Kaziranga.

Start your day early with an optional elephant safari to explore one of the zones of Kaziranga National Park, where you can witness the incredible wildlife up close, including the famed one-horned rhinoceros, along with a variety of other animals and bird species. After the safari, return to your hotel and enjoy a delicious, energizing breakfast. Later in the afternoon, embark on an exciting jeep safari—included in your package—offering a deeper exploration of the park’s diverse landscapes and rich biodiversity. After an adventurous day, return to your hotel and unwind, enjoying a peaceful overnight stay in Kaziranga.

After a hearty breakfast, begin your scenic journey to Bomdila, located at an altitude of 8,200 feet, transitioning from the lush green plains of Assam to the misty, forested mountains of Arunachal Pradesh. Upon arrival, check in to your hotel and take some time to relax. In the evening, visit the Gontse Rabgyaling Monastery (Upper Gonpa) and the Thub-Chog Gatsel Ling Monastery (Lower Gonpa), both offering a serene spiritual experience and stunning views of the surrounding hills. Enjoy a peaceful overnight stay in Bomdila.

After a delightful morning breakfast, embark on a mesmerizing road journey to Tawang, enjoying breathtaking views along the way. En route, experience the awe-inspiring beauty of the snow-capped Sela Pass, situated at an altitude of 14,000 feet, and pay homage at the Jaswant Garh War Memorial, a tribute to the bravery of soldiers who fought in the 1962 Indo-China war. Upon arrival in Tawang, check in to your hotel and relax. The evening is free for you to unwind at your own pace. Enjoy a comfortable overnight stay in Tawang.

Tawang/1747656106556-Vivanta Arunachal Pradesh, Tawang 1.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Tawang Local Sightseeing","day_summary":"

After a delicious breakfast, begin your day with a visit to the magnificent Tawang Monastery, the largest in India and a spiritual beacon of the region. Continue to Urgelling Gompa, the revered birthplace of the 6th Dalai Lama, steeped in Buddhist heritage, followed by a stop at the poignant Tawang War Memorial, honoring the brave soldiers of the 1962 Indo-China war. Later, explore the Tawang Craft Centre, where you can browse and purchase exquisite local handicrafts, including vibrant masks, handwoven carpets, and intricately designed silver, coral, and amber jewelry. Spend the evening at your leisure, soaking in the peaceful atmosphere, and enjoy an overnight stay in Tawang.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Tawang Local Sightseeing- High Altitude Lakes and Bumla Pass","day_summary":"

Begin your day with a delicious breakfast before setting out on a scenic drive to the enchanting Shonga-tser Lake, famously known as Madhuri Lake, surrounded by stunning Himalayan landscapes. Continue to the serene Sangetsar Lake, nestled amidst snow-capped mountains and tranquil surroundings—an ideal spot to enjoy some hot Maggi and momos while observing nearby war bunkers. The adventure continues with a drive to the historic Bumla Pass, located about 37 km from Tawang, near the Indo-China border. On your return journey, stop for photos at the breathtaking PTSO Lake, known for its crystal-clear waters and dramatic setting. Arrive back at your hotel in the afternoon, and spend your evening at leisure. Enjoy a restful overnight stay in Tawang.

Wake up to a delightful breakfast before beginning your scenic drive to Dirang, nestled at an altitude of 1,560 meters amidst picturesque valleys and mountain vistas. Upon arrival, check in to your hotel and take some time to relax. In the afternoon, visit the region’s apple and kiwi farms (season permitting), offering a glimpse into local agricultural life. Continue your exploration with peaceful visits to the Dirang Monastery and the serene Kalachakra Monastery, both reflecting the rich Buddhist heritage of the area. Enjoy a tranquil overnight stay in Dirang.

Transfer to Bhalukpong, located at the border of Assam and Arunachal Pradesh, enjoying the stunning views of the Brahmaputra River as you cross the impressive 1.4 km-long river bridge. Upon arrival, check in to your hotel and take some time to relax. In the evening, you can visit Kalakshetra to experience an enchanting light and sound show, or opt for a Golden Sunset Cruise on the Brahmaputra (available at your own direct expense) to soak in the breathtaking river views. Enjoy an overnight stay in Guwahati.

packages.overview:
• Unleash your wanderlust and explore the green wonder of Northeast India, Meghalaya with a memorable road journey.
  • Explore the mesmerizing waterfalls roaring and falling in the lush and steep green valley of Meghalaya.
  • Explore the Northeastern place referred to as the “Orchid State of India” with 600 plus species nurturing in it.
  • Bless your eyes with a stunning view of Umiam Lake, a majestic-man made reservoir that expands over an area of 222 square kilometres.
  • Stop over at the oldest and the second-largest monastery in the world, the Tawang Monastery standing tall with colorful prayer flags and panoramic views.

[{"city":"Shillong","country":"India","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/India/Shillong/1744984652303-AdobeStock_279618343.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Shillong/1744984652453-AdobeStock_290486921.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Shillong/1744984652569-AdobeStock_723953201.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/India/Shillong/1744984652633-AdobeStock_788818582.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Arrival in Guwahati - Transfer to Shillong - Leisure Day","day_summary":"

Welcome to Guwahati! Upon arrival at the Guwahati Airport, railway station, or bus stand, you will be warmly greeted by our TravelXploria Representative, who will assist you with a comfortable transfer to your hotel in Shillong, the charming capital of Meghalaya. En route, enjoy a stop at the breathtaking Umiam Lake, a serene man-made reservoir surrounded by rolling green hills, perfect for photography and relaxation. Once you arrive in Shillong, check in at your hotel and unwind. Later, you may choose to explore the city at your own pace—visit the tranquil Phan Nonglait Park, known for its beautifully maintained gardens and peaceful atmosphere, or head to the iconic Elephant Falls, a stunning three-tiered waterfall nestled in lush greenery, renowned for its natural beauty and soothing ambiance.

In the morning, embark on a city tour with a comfortable transfer arranged by Travelxploria. Begin your day at the All Saints Cathedral, a beautiful example of colonial-era Gothic architecture, known for its serene ambiance and historical charm. Then, visit the tranquil Ward’s Lake, a picturesque man-made lake surrounded by lush gardens—perfect for a leisurely stroll or paddle boating. Continue to the renowned Don Bosco Museum, one of the finest museums in Northeast India, offering fascinating insights into the region’s rich cultural diversity through its impressive multi-floor exhibits. Conclude your day at the breathtaking Laitlum Canyon, where you'll be captivated by sweeping views and dramatic cliffs that showcase the raw, untouched beauty of Meghalaya.

After check-out, embark on a scenic drive to Bhalukpong, a picturesque town located at the border of Assam and Arunachal Pradesh. Upon arrival, our Travelxploria representative will assist you with a smooth check-in at your hotel. The rest of the day is yours to enjoy at leisure. You may choose to visit the Pakhui Wildlife Sanctuary, a haven for nature lovers, renowned for its rich biodiversity, including rare species like tigers and hornbills, set amidst lush forested terrain. Alternatively, explore the historic Bhalukpong Fort, known for its ancient ruins and scenic surroundings, offering a fascinating glimpse into the region’s cultural and architectural heritage.

to Tawang - Enroute Sightseeing","day_summary":"

After check-out, begin your scenic journey to Tawang with a comfortable transfer arranged by Travelxploria. Along the way, marvel at the stunning landscapes as you cross the majestic Sela Pass, one of the highest motorable roads in the world. Pause at the serene Sela Lake, a pristine high-altitude lake that beautifully mirrors the sky, offering a perfect spot for reflection and photography. Continue your journey with a visit to the Jaswant Garh War Memorial, a moving tribute to the bravery of Rifleman Jaswant Singh Rawat, whose heroic stand during the 1962 Indo-China war remains an inspiring tale. Further along the route, stop at the enchanting Nuranang Falls, where the gushing waters and mist-covered surroundings create a truly magical atmosphere. Upon arrival in Tawang, check in to your hotel and relax for the night, preparing for the adventures ahead in this culturally rich Himalayan town.

Begin your Tawang city tour with a visit to the majestic Tawang Monastery, the largest monastery in India and a beacon of spiritual tranquility, renowned for its impressive architecture and serene ambiance. Next, head to Urgelling Gompa, the revered birthplace of the 6th Dalai Lama, steeped in spiritual and historical significance. Continue to the Tawang War Memorial, a solemn tribute to the brave soldiers who sacrificed their lives during the 1962 Indo-China war. Conclude your day with a stroll through the local Tawang market, where you can browse and shop for traditional treasures such as colorful masks, intricately woven carpets, and beautifully handcrafted silver and coral jewelry, capturing the rich culture and artistry of the region.

After check out, get driven to Bomdila. Upon arrival, check in to your hotel and spend your time at leisure. You can visit Gontse Rabgyaling Monastery (Upper Gonpa), known for its vibrant murals and peaceful hilltop setting. You can also explore Thub-Chog Gatsel Ling Monastery (Lower Gonpa), admired for its intricate architecture and deep spiritual significance in Tibetan Buddhism. In the evening, you can explore the local market, quaint cafes and streetlife of the town.

After check-out, embark on a scenic drive to Guwahati with a comfortable transfer arranged by Travelxploria. Enjoy picturesque views along the way, especially as you cross the majestic Brahmaputra River via one of the region’s iconic long-span bridges. Upon arrival in Guwahati, check in to your hotel and relax. The rest of the day is at your leisure—you may choose to visit the Guwahati Planetarium, known for its captivating astronomy shows and immersive stargazing experiences. Alternatively, if time permits, you can opt for a visit to the renowned Kaziranga National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site celebrated for its thriving population of one-horned rhinoceroses, diverse wildlife, and exhilarating jungle safaris amidst lush grasslands and wetlands.

Start your Guwahati city tour with a visit to the sacred Kamakhya Temple, one of the most revered Shakti Peeths and a powerful spiritual center perched atop the Nilachal Hills, offering panoramic views and deep religious significance. Continue to the Assam State Museum, where a remarkable collection of rare manuscripts, sculptures, and artifacts beautifully narrate the cultural and historical legacy of Assam. Next, head to the Assam State Zoo, home to a variety of rare and endangered species, and don’t miss the vibrant orchid garden within the premises. Then, explore the Regional Science Centre, an engaging space filled with interactive exhibits and scientific wonders perfect for all ages. Conclude your day with a serene visit to the Umananda Temple, located on Peacock Island in the middle of the Brahmaputra River, offering a peaceful spiritual retreat amidst stunning natural surroundings.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Departure Day","day_summary":"

In the morning, check out from your hotel and board your comfortable transfer to the Guwahati Airport, railway station, or bus stand, as arranged by Travelxploria. As you begin your return journey home, carry with you a heart full of unforgettable experiences and a bag full of cherished memories from your incredible adventure through Northeast India.

packages.overview:
Arunachal Pradesh, the beautiful mountains, neglected passes, quiet lakes and renowned monasteries is a stunning location for an astounding occasion. In the event that you wish to investigate the ancestral culture and the straightforwardness of their impeccable excellence. Explore a stunning cluster of greenery, in a novel territory with ice sheets, high height knolls and subtropical backwoods add to the appeal of the wonderful state in 6 days Arunachal Tour package. Have the most experiential tour and take away lots of stories to share with your family.

  • Start the 6 Days Arunachal pradesh tour from Guwahati to Bhalukpong via the most amazing road and crossing the river brahmaputra.
  • Travel through the roads passing in between the lush green valley and small enchanted villages of Arunachal pradesh.
  • See the town covered with valley view small stone houses coloured with white and red colours.
  • Check the stunning contrast of the natural colour palette in the quaint town of tawang.
  • In the 6 Days Arunachal Pradesh tour one shall witness the snow cape mountains dense emerald jungle and zigzag roads with colourful gompa.
  • Take a beautiful postcard perfect picture along with amazing memories to share.

packages.overview:
Arunachal Pradesh is perhaps the most delightful spot to investigate in the whole North East and is home to probably the most amazing and grand scenes in the country. Cover the high altitude passes, once from where the old lama entered the country. See the most stunning lake surrounded by the green mountains touch the blue sky. With its moving green slopes, blended culture and the endowment of Brahmaputra, this Arunachal Pradesh visit bundle presents to you the best of the state. Come and explore the biggest state amongst its seven northeastern sisters with us in 7 days Arunachal Tour Package.

  • Travel through the 3 km long river bridge settled upon river brahmaputra connecting Assam to Arunachal Pradesh.
  • Drive through the valley toward Dirang where a colourful orchidurin awaits 500 plus varieties of orchids are grown.
  • Cross the high altitude pass and lake surrounded with breathtaking mountain views and give the entry to Tawang.
  • Say your prayers in one of the oldest monetary systems of Asia and seek the blessings.
  • Walk through the oldest suspension bridge over the river stream adding to the beauty of the valley.
  • Drive toward the picturesque lake and pass, roads covered with colourful prayer flags adding to the beauty of the land.
  • Take a lot of wonderful memories along the way along with souvenirs from markets.

After breakfast, proceed on a scenic drive to Dirang. En route, stop at Tipi, located on the banks of the Bharali River at an elevation of 190 meters, renowned for its Orchidarium housing over 500 varieties of orchids. Continue your journey with a brief visit to the sacred Nag Temple before arriving in Dirang. Upon arrival, check in to your hotel and later explore the seasonal Apple and Kiwi farms, followed by visits to the serene Dirang Monastery and Kalachakra Monastery. Overnight stay in Dirang.

After breakfast, begin your journey to Tawang, covering approximately 130 km through the picturesque landscapes of Arunachal Pradesh. En route, marvel at the breathtaking views of Sela Pass and the serene Sela Lake. Continue with a short detour to visit the Jaswant Garh War Memorial, a tribute to the bravery of soldiers, followed by a stop at the stunning Nuranang Falls. Upon arrival in Tawang, check in to your hotel and enjoy the evening at leisure. Overnight stay in Tawang.

After breakfast, set out for a full day of local sightseeing in Tawang. You may opt to visit the iconic Bum La Pass, located at an elevation of 5,000 meters along the Indo-China border, and witness the historic route taken by the Dalai Lama when he entered India. Continue your journey with a visit to the picturesque Shonga-tser Lake, also known as Madhuri Lake, followed by Taktsang Gompa. Later, explore the magnificent Tawang Monastery, the largest Buddhist monastery in India. In the evening, stroll through the vibrant local markets. Overnight stay in Tawang.

packages.overview:
• Copenhagen, Denmark – Explore the colorful Nyhavn waterfront, visit the Little Mermaid statue, stroll through Tivoli Gardens, and admire the Rosenborg Castle.
  • Stockholm, Sweden – Discover the beauty of the Swedish capital, with attractions like Gamla Stan (Old Town), the Royal Palace, the Vasa Museum, and enjoy a scenic cruise through the Stockholm Archipelago.
  • Helsinki, Finland – Experience the blend of modern design and historic charm, visiting sites such as Helsinki Cathedral, Suomenlinna Fortress, and the vibrant Market Square.
  • Tallinn, Estonia – Step back in time in Tallinn’s medieval Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, featuring cobblestone streets, gothic churches, and fairy-tale architecture.

Welcome to your unforgettable journey with Travelxploria! As soon as you arrive, our dedicated Travelxploria representative will be there to greet you with a warm welcome and ensure a smooth transfer to your hotel. Take some time to relax and settle in, or explore your surroundings at your own pace. In the afternoon, our representative will be available to provide you with all the details you need for the adventure ahead. You can also find important tour information displayed on the boards at the hotel reception.

In the morning, we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of the Danish capital with a local guide. Copenhagen, the largest Scandinavian city, is full of contrasts, featuring the popular Tivoli Gardens, Christiansborg Palace, the charming terraces of Nyhavn, and the iconic Little Mermaid statue. In the late morning, we’ll journey to Jutland. Our route includes crossing a 20 km bridge over the sea and stopping at Odense, where we can explore its Gothic cathedral and H.C. Andersen’s House. Upon arrival in Aarhus, you’ll have free time.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Copenhagen","to":"AARHUS","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":695,"h_name":"Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham","h_country":"Denmark","h_city":"AARHUS","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562158-unnamed (59).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562232-2021-04-14.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/AARHUS/Comwell Aarhus, Dolce by Wyndham/1741156562273-unnamed (58).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Stavanger","country":"Norway","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Aarhus to Kristiansand by Kristiansand Ferry & Stay at Stavanger","day_summary":"

We’ll travel to Hirtshals and take a ferry across the North Sea to Norway, embarking on a three-hour voyage to Kristiansand. From there, we’ll continue along Norway’s stunning southern coastline. Our next stop will be the charming little town of Flekkefjord. We’ll then arrive in Stavanger, a picturesque town with a bustling port by the fjord.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Ferry","from":"AARHUS","to":"Stavanger","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":697,"h_name":"Clarion Hotel Energy","h_country":"Norway","h_city":"Stavanger","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Stavanger/Clarion Hotel Energy/1741157572875-unnamed (63).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Stavanger/Clarion Hotel Energy/1741157572952-Cl_Energy_RM_13_standard_enkel.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Stavanger/Clarion Hotel Energy/1741157572991-unnamed (62).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Bergen","country":"Norway","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Bergen/1742150469947-AdobeStock_187978352-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Bergen/1742150472633-AdobeStock_189721277-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Bergen/1742150473321-AdobeStock_297919201-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Stavanger to Bergen","day_summary":"

Upon leaving the city, we’ll stop at the Swords in Rock monument, a tribute to the Viking era, before continuing our scenic route through tunnels, bridges, and some segments by boat along the fjord coast of Norway. One of these undersea tunnels is the deepest in the world. The two boat trips will make this part of the journey both relaxing and spectacular. We’ll arrive in Bergen at lunchtime and take a cable car up Mount Fløyen for amazing views of the town and the Bergen fjord. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore the capital city of fjords.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Stavanger","to":"Bergen","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":699,"h_name":"Scandic Neptun","h_country":"Norway","h_city":"Bergen","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Bergen/Scandic Neptun/1741157889308-2021-10-16.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Bergen/Scandic Neptun/1741157889416-unnamed (67).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Bergen/Scandic Neptun/1741157889425-unnamed.png\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Oslo","country":"Norway","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149250639-AdobeStock_67424562-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149251103-AdobeStock_101002987-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149251252-AdobeStock_120502702-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Norway/Oslo/1742149251569-AdobeStock_329111106-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Bergen to Sognefjord & Stay at Oslo","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll experience more breathtaking landscapes, including Norway’s fjords, glaciers, lakes, and forests. Our journey will take us to Sognefjord, the largest fjord in Norway. Along the way, we’ll stop at the stunning Tvindefossen Waterfall. Then, we’ll embark on a magnificent cruise between Gudvangen and Aurland, enjoying the stunning scenery during this hour-and-a-half voyage. We suggest having lunch on baord. Continuing our adventure, we’ll traverse magnificent snow-covered landscapes and pass through the Aurland Tunnel, the longest in the world. We’ll visit Borgund’s 12th-century wooden church, known as Norway’s best-preserved medieval church (admission included). Finally, we’ll arrive in Oslo with free time to explore the port, main streets, and the artwork in Frogner Park.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Bergen","to":"Oslo","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":702,"h_name":"SCANDIC HELSFYR","h_country":"Norway","h_city":"Oslo","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Oslo/SCANDIC HELSFYR/1741158205917-unnamed (74).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Oslo/SCANDIC HELSFYR/1741158205993-unnamed (1).png\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Oslo/SCANDIC HELSFYR/1741158206076-unnamed (73).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Stockholm","country":"Sweden","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Sweden/Stockholm/1742148627486-AdobeStock_120699499-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Sweden/Stockholm/1742148628111-AdobeStock_371412259-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Sweden/Stockholm/1742148628272-AdobeStock_441649606-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Sweden/Stockholm/1742148628407-AdobeStock_652792822-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Oslo to Orebro & Stay at Stockholm","day_summary":"

We include a panoramic tour of the Norwegian capital with a local guide. This city blends vibrant modern architecture with classical buildings, stunning scenery, and large parks with sculptures. Oslo has been named the European Green Capital. After the tour, you´ll have free time to explore on your own.

Next, we’ll depart for Sweden, passing through landscapes of forests and lakes. Our next stop is Örebro, a historic town by a beautiful lake, with a castle and a charming old town. Enjoy some time for a stroll before we continue to Stockholm, arriving in the evening.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Oslo","to":"Stockholm","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":672,"h_name":"QUALITY HOTEL GLOB","h_country":"Sweden","h_city":"Stockholm","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Stockholm/QUALITY HOTEL GLOB/1741094725143-3J8A5829.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Stockholm/QUALITY HOTEL GLOB/1741094725244-2024-02-29.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Stockholm/QUALITY HOTEL GLOB/1741094725250-unnamed (30).jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Stockholm City Tour","day_summary":"

Today we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of this beautiful capital city, built on 14 interconnected islands. We’ll visit Gamla Stan, also known as the “the city between bridges”, the lively old quarter filled with monumental buildings such as the Nobel Prize Museum, located right beside the main square of Stortorget which honors Nobel Prize winners, the Cathedral, and the Royal Palace. Whenever possible, we’ll enjoy the stunning views from the Fjällgatan viewpoint. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore. We recommend visiting the museums and parks. Optionally, you can visit the City Hall and the Vasa Museum.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Stockholm Leisure at Day & Turku Ferry in the Evening","day_summary":"

Enjoy a free day in Stockholm. In the evening, we’ll board a modern ferry for an overnight journey to Finland. Accommodation will be in double en-suite cabins.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Helsinki","country":"Finland","banner_image":[{"path":"/Destination/Finland/Helsinki/1742147811227-AdobeStock_56312148-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Finland/Helsinki/1742147812583-AdobeStock_246660981-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Finland/Helsinki/1742147812722-AdobeStock_306012807-min.jpeg"},{"path":"/Destination/Finland/Helsinki/1742147812834-AdobeStock_422497458-min.jpeg"}],"day_details":[{"day_title":"Stockholm to Helsinki","day_summary":"

As we arrive in Finland, we recommend heading to the ship’s deck for sunrise to enjoy the mesmerizing landscapes as we sail past the Åland Islands, a collection of hundreds of forested islands. After disembarking the ferry, you’ll have time to explore Turku, a city with a strong Swedish influence. We’ll then continue our journey to the neighboring city of Naantali, a picturesque town with charming wooden houses by the sea. Our travels will take us to Helsinki, where we’ll arrive around lunchtime. In the afternoon, you’ll have leisure time to explore the city, including its old market, Russian-influenced buildings, and beautiful churches.

We’ll embark on an included boat trip to the Suomenlinna Islands, six islands home to a magnificent fortress that once protected the city’s entrance and is now a World Heritage Site. Take some time to explore, crossing the islands via pedestrian bridges. In the afternoon, we’ll board a ferry to Estonia, a journey of about 2 hours and 30 minutes. As we depart from Helsinki, you’ll enjoy stunning views of the surrounding islands. In the evening, we’ll arrive in Tallinn.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Ferry","from":"Helsinki","to":"Tallinn","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":792,"h_name":"Park Inn by Radisson Meriton Conference & Spa Hotel Tallinn","h_country":"Estonia","h_city":"Tallinn","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Tallinn/Park Inn by Radisson Meriton Conference & Spa Hotel Tallinn/1741590256805-2024-10-27.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Tallinn/Park Inn by Radisson Meriton Conference & Spa Hotel Tallinn/1741590256895-unnamed - 2025-03-10T123217.824.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Tallinn/Park Inn by Radisson Meriton Conference & Spa Hotel Tallinn/1741590256896-2024-05-16 (2).jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Tallinn City Tour","day_summary":"

The capital of Estonia is a wonderful city that has preserved its medieval core intact. You´ll enjoy a panoramic tour of the city, its walls, churches, squares, and its medieval streets filled with tourist attractions. Free time in the afternoon.

","day_element":[]},{"day_title":"Departure Day","day_summary":"

After a delightful breakfast, it’s time to bid farewell to this incredible journey. Our Travelxploria representative will be there to assist you with any final arrangements, ensuring a smooth departure. As you reflect on the breathtaking landscapes, rich history, and unforgettable experiences, we hope you carry these cherished memories with you for a lifetime.

packages.package_name: Timeless Europe A Tour of History Art and Nature
packages.overview:
• Frankfurt, Germany: A dynamic financial hub with a blend of modern skyscrapers and historic architecture. Visit the Römer, St. Bartholomew's Cathedral, and explore the charming old town (Altstadt).

  • Dortmund, Germany: Known for its football culture and industrial heritage, Dortmund offers attractions like the Signal Iduna Park, the German Football Museum, and the Westfalenpark with its stunning views.

  • Hanover, Germany: A city rich in history and culture, home to magnificent gardens like Herrenhausen Gardens, the Lower Saxony State Museum, and a lively arts scene.

  • Aarhus, Denmark: A city where the past meets the future, with attractions like the ARoS Aarhus Art Museum, the Old Town (Den Gamle By), and vibrant waterfront areas.

  • Stavanger, Norway: A coastal city famous for its stunning natural beauty, including the famous Preikestolen (Pulpit Rock). Explore its charming old town and fascinating Viking history.

  • Bergen, Norway: Nestled between mountains and fjords, Bergen offers picturesque streets, the UNESCO World Heritage-listed Bryggen, and access to stunning fjord cruises.

  • Oslo, Norway: Norway’s capital is known for its vibrant cultural scene, including the Viking Ship Museum, the Munch Museum, and the beautiful Oslofjord, offering a perfect mix of urban life and nature.

  • Stockholm, Sweden: A city spread across 14 islands, known for its beautiful architecture, world-class museums like the Vasa Museum, and vibrant district of Gamla Stan (Old Town).

  • Copenhagen, Denmark: Denmark’s capital is a mix of royal history, modern design, and Scandinavian charm. Must-visit attractions include Tivoli Gardens, Nyhavn, and the iconic Little Mermaid statue.

  • Berlin, Germany: A city that blends history and modernity, Berlin is known for its rich cultural heritage, the Berlin Wall remnants, Brandenburg Gate, Museum Island, and a vibrant nightlife scene.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Frankfurt Airport","to":"Frankfurt","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":690,"h_name":"MARITIM FRANKFURT","h_country":"Germany","h_city":"Frankfurt","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Frankfurt/MARITIM FRANKFURT/1741155474506-2022-07-04.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Frankfurt/MARITIM FRANKFURT/1741155474588-2023-01-26.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Frankfurt/MARITIM FRANKFURT/1741155474629-unnamed (53).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Dortmund","country":"Germany","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Departure from Frankfurt with Cruise on the Rhine & Dortmund Arrival","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll explore the Rhine and Moselle Valleys, two rivers full of history and dotted with charming villages. We´ll begin with a short cruise on the Rhine, between the villages of Rüdesheim and Sankt Goar, undoubtedly the most picturesque section of the river. Next, we’ll head to the Moselle River, stopping for lunch in Cochem, a picturesque village on the Rhine riverbank overlooked by its imposing castle, which dates back over a thousand years. Afterward, we’ll travel to Cologne, where you´ll have time to visit its impressive Gothic cathedral. Standing at 154 meters tall, it was the tallest building in the world until the late 19th century. You can also enjoy a walk along the Rhine River and through the lively city center before we continue to our hotel in Dortmund.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Frankfurt","to":"Dortmund","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":691,"h_name":"MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM","h_country":"Germany","h_city":"Dortmund","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Dortmund/MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM/1741155695447-LogoEingangMercureDortmundCentrum - komprimiert.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Dortmund/MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM/1741155695481-2024-10-15.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Dortmund/MERCURE DORTMUND CENTRUM/1741155695551-2024-11-19.jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Hanover","country":"Germany","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Dortmund to Hanover & Stay at Hanover","day_summary":"

Today, we’ll embark on a magical journey, reminiscing about our favorite childhood stories and movies as we travel through the towns that inspired the Brothers Grimm, now known as the Fairy Tale Route. Our first stop will be Trendelburg, a picturesque walled village where we’ll visit the large medieval castle and the famous Rapunzel Tower (admission included).


We’ll continue along scenic roads, crossing forests that will remind us of the tale of Hansel and Gretel. Our next stop will be Höxter, a vibrant town where we’ll enjoy lunch and visit Corvey Abbey, an ancient 9th-century monastery and World Heritage Site (admission included). We’ll then make a brief stop in Polle to photograph the exterior ruins of Cinderella’s Castle. Our journey will continue to Hamelin, renowned for the tale of “The Pied Piper of Hamelin”. After following the mouse tracks, we’ll proceed to Hanover, where we’ll have time to explore the city center and take a stroll next to the old town hall.

One of these undersea tunnels is the deepest in the world. The two boat trips will make this part of the journey both relaxing and spectacular. We’ll arrive in Bergen at lunchtime and take a cable car up Mount Fløyen for amazing views of the town and the Bergen fjord. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore the capital city of fjords.

Today, we’ll experience more breathtaking landscapes, including Norway’s fjords, glaciers, lakes, and forests. Our journey will take us to Sognefjord, the largest fjord in Norway. Along the way, we’ll stop at the stunning Tvindefossen Waterfall. Then, we’ll embark on a magnificent cruise between Gudvangen and Aurland, enjoying the stunning scenery during this hour-and-a-half voyage. Continuing our adventure, we’ll traverse magnificent snow-covered landscapes and pass through the Aurland Tunnel, the longest in the world. We’ll visit the exterior of Borgund’s 12th-century wooden church, known as Norway’s best-preserved medieval church. Finally, we’ll arrive in Oslo with free time to explore the port, main streets, and the artwork in Frogner Park.

We include a panoramic tour of the Norwegian capital with a local guide. This city blends vibrant modern architecture with classical buildings, stunning scenery, and large parks with sculptures. Oslo has been named the European Green Capital. After the tour, you´ll have free time to explore on your own.

Next, we’ll depart for Sweden, passing through landscapes of forests and lakes. Our next stop is Örebro, a historic town by a beautiful lake, with a castle and a charming old town. Enjoy some time for a stroll before we continue to Stockholm, arriving in the evening.

Today we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of this beautiful capital city, built on 14 interconnected islands. We’ll visit Gamla Stan, also known as the “the city between bridges”, the lively old quarter filled with monumental buildings such as the Nobel Prize Museum, located right beside the main square of Stortorget which honors Nobel Prize winners, the Cathedral, and the Royal Palace. Whenever possible, we’ll enjoy the stunning views from the Fjällgatan viewpoint. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore. We recommend visiting the museums and parks. Optionally, you can visit the City Hall and the Vasa Museum.

packages.package_name: From Frankfurt to Copenhagen: A Pan-European Odyssey
packages.overview:
• Frankfurt – A dynamic blend of modern skyscrapers and historic charm welcomes you to this vibrant German metropolis.
  • Rhine River Cruise – Sail through fairy-tale landscapes, passing medieval castles and lush vineyards along the picturesque riverbanks.
  • Cochem Castle – A majestic fortress perched above the Moselle River, offering breathtaking views and medieval charm.
  • Cologne Cathedral – Marvel at this towering Gothic masterpiece, once the world’s tallest building, dominating the city skyline.
  • Fairy Tale Route – Wander through storybook villages, visiting Rapunzel’s Tower and the legendary Pied Piper’s Hamelin.
  • Hamburg – Discover Germany’s lively port city, with its striking architecture, bustling waterfront, and maritime heritage.
  • Stavanger – Stroll through this picturesque Norwegian town, renowned for its fjordside charm and Viking history.
  • Bergen & Fløyen Viewpoint – Ride the cable car for panoramic views of Norway’s stunning fjords and colorful harbor.
  • Sognefjord Cruise – Glide through Norway’s largest and most spectacular fjord, surrounded by majestic mountains and waterfalls.
  • Oslo & Frogner Park – Explore Norway’s stylish capital, home to grand palaces, modern architecture, and Vigeland’s iconic sculptures.
  • Stockholm & Gamla Stan – Discover Sweden’s enchanting capital, built on 14 islands, with cobbled streets and royal grandeur.
  • Helsingor & Kronborg Castle – Step into Shakespeare’s world at Hamlet’s legendary castle on the Danish coastline.
  • Copenhagen & Nyhavn – Experience Denmark’s capital, where colorful harbor houses and royal landmarks exude Nordic charm.

Take some time to relax or explore the surroundings at your own pace. In the afternoon, feel free to check the hotel reception boards for important details about the start of your exciting tour.

Today, we’ll explore the Rhine and Moselle Valleys, two rivers full of history and dotted with charming villages. We´ll begin with a short cruise on the Rhine, between the villages of Rüdesheim and Sankt Goar, undoubtedly the most picturesque section of the river. Next, we’ll head to the Moselle River, stopping for lunch in Cochem, a picturesque village on the Rhine riverbank overlooked by its imposing castle, which dates back over a thousand years. Afterward, we’ll travel to Cologne, where you´ll have time to visit its impressive Gothic cathedral. Standing at 154 meters tall, it was the tallest building in the world until the late 19th century. You can also enjoy a walk along the Rhine River and through the lively city center before we continue to our hotel in Dortmund.

Today, we’ll embark on a magical journey, reminiscing about our favorite childhood stories and movies as we travel through the towns that inspired the Brothers Grimm, now known as the Fairy Tale Route. Our first stop will be Trendelburg, a picturesque walled village where we’ll visit the large medieval castle and the famous Rapunzel Tower (admission included).



We’ll continue along scenic roads, crossing forests that will remind us of the tale of Hansel and Gretel. Our next stop will be Höxter, a vibrant town where we’ll enjoy lunch and visit Corvey Abbey, an ancient 9th-century monastery and World Heritage Site (admission included). We’ll then make a brief stop in Polle to photograph the exterior ruins of Cinderella’s Castle. Our journey will continue to Hamelin, renowned for the tale of “The Pied Piper of Hamelin”. After following the mouse tracks, we’ll proceed to Hanover, where we’ll have time to explore the city center and take a stroll next to the old town hall.

As you depart, soak in the last glimpses of the mountains and coastal landscapes that define western Norway. The route to Oslo offers stunning views of lush valleys, deep fjords, and picturesque countryside. Upon arrival in Oslo, the vibrant capital welcomes you with its modern architecture, historic landmarks, and cultural richness. This transition marks the perfect blend of Norway’s natural wonders and urban elegance, offering a complete Nordic experience.

packages.package_name: Baltic Delight Capitals Coastlines and Culture
packages.overview:
Tallinn, Estonia – A Fairytale Capital

  • Wander through Tallinn’s Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage site with cobblestone streets and medieval towers.

  • Visit Toompea Castle and enjoy panoramic views from the Kohtuotsa Viewing Platform.

  • Explore the Alexander Nevsky Cathedral, an architectural gem with Russian Orthodox influences.

  • Stroll along the lively Town Hall Square, lined with charming cafes and shops.

2. Riga, Latvia – Art Nouveau & Culture

  • Marvel at the stunning Art Nouveau architecture in the city center.

  • Discover the historic Riga Old Town, home to the House of the Blackheads and St. Peter’s Church.

  • Visit the bustling Central Market, one of Europe’s largest food markets.

  • Walk along the Daugava River Promenade for scenic views and relaxation.

3. Vilnius, Lithuania – A Blend of Cultures

  • Explore the Gothic and Baroque architecture in Vilnius Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

  • Visit the Gediminas Tower, offering breathtaking views of the city.

  • Discover the Uzupis District, a bohemian enclave known for its artistic vibe.

  • Experience the spiritual atmosphere at the Gates of Dawn, a historic religious site.

4. Helsinki, Finland – The Nordic Gem

  • Visit the stunning Helsinki Cathedral, a neoclassical landmark.

  • Explore the vibrant Market Square, famous for its local delicacies.

  • Take a ferry to the Suomenlinna Fortress, a UNESCO-listed sea fortress.

  • Experience modern Nordic design at the Oodi Library and Temppeliaukio Church (Rock Church).

5. Warsaw, Poland – A Historic Capital

  • Walk through the beautifully reconstructed Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage site.

  • Visit the Royal Castle and the Lazienki Park, home to the famous Chopin Monument.

  • Learn about Polish history at the Warsaw Uprising Museum.

  • Stroll along Krakowskie Przedmieście, one of Warsaw’s most elegant streets.

After disembarking the ferry, you’ll have time to explore Turku, a city with a strong Swedish influence. We’ll then continue our journey to the neighboring city of Naantali, a picturesque town with charming wooden houses by the sea. Our travels will take us to Helsinki, where we’ll arrive around lunchtime. In the afternoon, you’ll have leisure time to explore the city, including its old market, Russian-influenced buildings, and beautiful churches.

We’ll travel through forests and plains to reach Parnu, a popular seaside resort. From there, we’ll continue to Latvia. You can enjoy an included lunch just before visiting the medieval Turaida Castle (admission included) surrounded by a large park with sculptures. Next, you can take a walk in Sigulda, a small and charming town situated amidst a beautiful landscape. Our journey will continue towards Riga, with a stop at the Salaspils Memorial, located at the site of a former concentration camp from the Nazi period. Since 1967, it has been one of the largest monumental complexes in Europe, commemorating the victims of this regime. We’ll arrive in Riga in the evening.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Vilnius ","to":"Warsaw","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":676,"h_name":"NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT","h_country":"Poland","h_city":"Warsaw","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT/1741095207837-IMG_20210905_074940_edited.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT/1741095207938-2020-11-07.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/NOVOTEL WARSZAWA AIRPORT/1741095207946-unnamed (39).jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Warsaw city tour ","day_summary":"

We’ll start the day with a panoramic tour of Warsaw with a local guide. Home to nearly two million people, this political and economic hub of Poland boasts a blend of modern architecture and meticulously restored historic buildings. We’ll stroll through the charming old town, featuring many old buildings, the Royal Palace, and cobbled streets, all restored after their destruction in World War II and now declared a World Heritage Site. We’ll visit the Palace of Culture, a skyscraper gifted by the Soviet Union, reflect on the history of what was once the largest Jewish ghetto in Europe, and wander through Lazienki Park, home to the Chopin Monument.


You’ll have free time in the afternoon to visit museums, such as the National Museum and the Warsaw Rising Museum, or to explore the lively city center filled with shops and activities.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Gdansk","country":"Poland","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Going to Gdansk ","day_summary":"

After breakfast, we’ll depart for northern Poland. Our first stop will be Olsztynek, where we’ll visit the open-air Museum of Folk Architecture (admission included). Here, you can admire wooden houses, old mills, and a traditional church, making it feel like you’ve stepped back in time! We’ll then continue to the Elblag Canal, the longest navigable canal in Poland, where we’ll visit a museum located in an area where boats are hauled over land on rails to navigate elevation changes. Next, we’ll head to Malbork to visit its impressive castle (admission included, green route), one of the largest medieval castles in Europe, built in the 13th century by the Teutonic Order and now a UNESCO World Heritage Site. We’ll arrive in Gdansk—formerly known as Danzig—in the evening. The historic center is stunning and lively, and we highly recommend an evening stroll to experience its vibrant atmosphere.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Warsaw","to":"Gdansk","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":795,"h_name":"Novotel Gdańsk Centrum","h_country":"Poland","h_city":"Gdansk","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Gdansk/Novotel Gdańsk Centrum/1741591326488-2024-12-23.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Gdansk/Novotel Gdańsk Centrum/1741591326637-unnamed - 2025-03-10T124945.908.jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Gdansk/Novotel Gdańsk Centrum/1741591326642-2024-07-01 (7).jpg\"}]"}]}}]}]},{"city":"Sopot","country":"Poland","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Setting off for Sopot ","day_summary":"

After breakfast, we’ll join our guide for a visit to the European Solidarity Centre, a striking contemporary building that commemorates the history of the Solidarity movement, which played a pivotal role in the fall of the Soviet Union. Highlights include the monument dedicated to the fallen workers. Next, we’ll include admission to the Museum of the Second World War. This impressive museum, opened in 2017, serves as a powerful reminder of the 3 to 6 million people who lost their lives in Poland during that tragic conflict. Afterward, we’ll have some time to explore the historic center before heading to Sopot, a charming seaside resort on the Baltic Sea. Known for its vibrant atmosphere, Sopot features a long sandy beach and one of Europe’s longest wooden piers, offering stunning views of the sea. In the evening, we’ll board a modern ferry (cruise-style) for our journey to Sweden. The scheduled departure is at 07:30 p.m., with overnight accommodations in cabins. Breakfast will be included

Stockholm, the capital of Sweden, is a beautiful city spread across 14 islands connected by bridges, blending historic charm with modern innovation. The medieval Old Town, Gamla Stan, features narrow cobblestone streets, colorful buildings, and landmarks like the Royal Palace. The city is renowned for its museums, including the Vasa Museum, ABBA Museum, and Skansen, as well as its vibrant art scene with metro stations known as underground galleries. Stockholm boasts a strong focus on sustainability, clean urban planning, and a rich café culture centered around the Swedish tradition of fika. Each district offers its own vibe, from the trendy streets of Södermalm to the upscale elegance of Östermalm. Surrounded by the Stockholm archipelago, the city also provides stunning natural escapes just a boat ride away. With friendly English-speaking locals, excellent public transport, and a peaceful atmosphere, Stockholm is an ideal destination for culture, nature, and design lovers alike.

packages.package_name: Baltics Wonders: From Stockholm to Warsaw
packages.overview:
• Stockholm – Explore Gamla Stan’s medieval charm, visit the Nobel Prize Museum, and admire the Royal Palace’s grandeur.
  • Overnight Ferry to Turku – Sail through the stunning Åland Islands and wake up to Finland’s scenic beauty.
  • Helsinki & Naantali – Stroll through Helsinki’s vibrant markets and Naantali’s picturesque seaside wooden houses.
  • Tallinn – Walk Tallinn’s enchanting medieval streets, surrounded by ancient city walls and historic landmarks.
  • Riga – Discover Riga’s Art Nouveau wonders and Soviet-era history through its captivating architecture.
  • Vilnius & Trakai – Admire Vilnius’ baroque skyline and visit Trakai Castle, an island fortress steeped in history.
  • Warsaw – Experience Warsaw’s rebuilt Old Town, historic ghetto memorials, and the grandeur of Lazienki Park.

​Today we’ll embark on a panoramic tour of this beautiful capital city, built on 14 interconnected islands. We’ll visit Gamla Stan, also known as the “the city between bridges”, the lively old quarter filled with monumental buildings such as the Nobel Prize Museum, located right beside the main square of Stortorget which honors Nobel Prize winners, the Cathedral, and the Royal Palace. Whenever possible, we’ll enjoy the stunning views from the Fjällgatan viewpoint. Afterwards, you’ll have leisure time to explore. We recommend visiting the museums and parks. Optionally, you can visit the City Hall and the Vasa Museum.


​

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Ferry","from":"Stockholm","to":"Helsinki","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":674,"h_name":"Scandic Grand Marina","h_country":"Finland","h_city":"Helsinki","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Helsinki/Scandic Grand Marina/1741094943349-unnamed (36).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Helsinki/Scandic Grand Marina/1741094943442-unnamed (35).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Helsinki/Scandic Grand Marina/1741094943485-unnamed (34).jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Visit to the Turku, Naantali, Helsinki","day_summary":"

As we arrive in Finland, we recommend heading to the ship’s deck for sunrise to enjoy the mesmerizing landscapes as we sail past the Åland Islands, a collection of hundreds of forested islands. After disembarking the ferry, you’ll have time to explore Turku, a city with a strong Swedish influence. We’ll then continue our journey to the neighboring city of Naantali, a picturesque town with charming wooden houses by the sea. Our travels will take us to Helsinki, where we’ll arrive around lunchtime. In the afternoon, you’ll have leisure time to explore the city, including its old market, Russian-influenced buildings, and beautiful churches.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Tallinn","country":"Estonia","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Experiencing Helsinki in Morning & Afternoon Transfer to Tallinn","day_summary":"

We’ll embark on an included boat trip to the Suomenlinna Islands, six islands home to a magnificent fortress that once protected the city’s entrance and is now a World Heritage Site. Take some time to explore, crossing the islands via pedestrian bridges. In the afternoon, we’ll board a ferry to Estonia, a journey of about 2 hours and 30 minutes. As we depart from Helsinki, you’ll enjoy stunning views of the surrounding islands. In the evening, we’ll arrive in Tallinn.

The capital of Estonia is a wonderful city that has preserved its medieval core intact. You´ll enjoy a panoramic tour of the city, its walls, churches, squares, and its medieval streets filled with tourist attractions. Free time in the afternoon.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Riga","country":"Latvia","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Departure from Tallinn & Arrival at Riga","day_summary":"

We’ll travel through forests and plains to reach Parnu, a popular seaside resort. From there, we’ll continue to Latvia. You can enjoy an included lunch just before visiting the medieval Turaida Castle (admission included) surrounded by a large park with sculptures. Next, you can take a walk in Sigulda, a small and charming town situated amidst a beautiful landscape. Our journey will continue towards Riga, with a stop at the Salaspils Memorial, located at the site of a former concentration camp from the Nazi period. Since 1967, it has been one of the largest monumental complexes in Europe, commemorating the victims of this regime. We’ll arrive in Riga in the evening.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Ferry","from":"Tallinn","to":"Riga","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":794,"h_name":"Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga","h_country":"Latvia","h_city":"Riga","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Riga/Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga/1741590631350-2025-02-23 (1).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Riga/Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga/1741590631476-2024-07-01 (6).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Riga/Radisson Blu Latvija Conference & Spa Hotel, Riga/1741590631519-2021-06-14.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"City Tour at Riga","day_summary":"

You’ll enjoy an included panoramic tour of this capital, the most populous city in the Baltic states, where traces of the Soviet era are still visible. Riga is renowned not only for its cultural center but also for its medieval Historic Center and Art Nouveau architecture. The afternoon will be free for you to explore at your leisure.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Vilnius ","country":"Lithuania","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Riga Departure & Arrival at Vilnius","day_summary":"

We’ll depart for Lithuania early in the morning. On our way there, we’ll visit the Baroque Palace of Rundale, built by the same architect who designed the Saint Petersburg’s Hermitage Museum. We’ll cross over into Lithuania. This country has a strong Catholic influence, highlighted by the impressive Hill of Crosses, with thousands of crosses of all sizes. Lunch will be included along our route. Afterwards, we’ll visit Trakai, a gorgeous castle located on an island in a lake, close to Vilnius. We’ll finish the day in Vilnius.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Riga","to":"Vilnius ","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":796,"h_name":"Radisson Blu Hotel Lietuva, Vilnius","h_country":"Lithuania","h_city":"Vilnius ","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Vilnius City Tour","day_summary":"

We’ll begin with a panoramic tour of the city built on seven hills, often compared to Rome. You’ll discover its Baroque churches, historic walls, grand castle, bustling commercial streets in the center, and 1,200 medieval houses. Enjoy free time in the afternoon to explore at your leisure.

","day_element":[]}]},{"city":"Warsaw","country":"Poland","banner_image":null,"day_details":[{"day_title":"Vilnius Departure & Arrival at Warsaw","day_summary":"

We’ll continue our journey to Poland. Our first stop will be Grutas Park, also known as “Stalin’s World,” which features 86 statues from the Soviet era. We’ll then stop for lunch in the charming lakeside town of Augustów. Afterward, we’ll head to central Poland, arriving in Warsaw by the end of the day.

","day_element":[{"type":"transfer","transferDetails":{"transferType":"Private","transferIn":"Cab","from":"Vilnius ","to":"Warsaw","splitStops":[]}},{"type":"hotel","groupedData":{"4":[{"h_id":675,"h_name":"RADISSON BLU SOBIESKI","h_country":"Poland","h_city":"Warsaw","h_type":"Hotel","h_rating":"4","status":"A","h_images":null,"supplier":null,"inserted_by_id":"Shouvik","edited_by_id":null,"price":null,"room_type":null,"images":"[{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/RADISSON BLU SOBIESKI/1741095111105-unnamed (38).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/RADISSON BLU SOBIESKI/1741095111174-unnamed (37).jpg\"},{\"path\":\"/Hotels/Warsaw/RADISSON BLU SOBIESKI/1741095111205-2021-10-21.jpg\"}]"}]}}]},{"day_title":"Warsaw Sightseeing & Departure in the Evening","day_summary":"

We’ll start the day with a panoramic tour of Warsaw with a local guide. Home to nearly two million people, this political and economic hub of Poland boasts a blend of modern architecture and meticulously restored historic buildings. We’ll stroll through the charming Old Town, featuring many old buildings, the Royal Palace, and cobbled streets, all restored after their destruction in World War II and now declared a World Heritage Site. We’ll visit the Palace of Culture, a skyscraper gifted by the Soviet Union, reflect on the history of what was once the largest Jewish ghetto in Europe, and wander through Lazienki Park, home to the Chopin Monument.



You’ll have free time in the afternoon to visit museums, such as the National Museum and the Warsaw Rising Museum, or to explore the lively city center filled with shops and activities.



Our journey will come to an end, leaving you with wonderful and lasting memories.

Please, check the time of your flight in case you might need an additional night.

{"title":"Swagata Chatterjee","rating":"5","publishDate":"2025-02-11","publishBy":"","subItems":[{"type":"text","content":"

Vietnam Tour from 11th Feb '25 to 20th Feb'25.

Vietnam tour was dream for us for long times. At last we could fulfill our dream with precise guidance of our tour operator Travelxploria.

We started our tour from Kolkata to Ho Chi Minh City (southern Vietnam) to Da Nang (Central Vietnam) and ended up at Hanoi (Northern Vietnam) to Kolkata, covering Cu Chi tunnels tour, Mackong Delta tour, Hop On Hop Off Ho Chi Minh City Bus tour, Marble Mountain tour, Hoi An Lantern Boat ride, Ba Na hill Golden Bridge, French Village tour, Da Nang Dragon Bridge Fire Show, Halong Bay Cruise night stay, Sampan Boat ride, Trung Trang Cave tour, Ninh Binh Ancient Capital tour, Tam Coc Boat ride, Hanoi Cycle Rickshaw city tour etc.

We have been accomodated in premium Hotels and Cruise and moreover liasoning of the tour itinerary in skill hands by our tour operator Travelxploria made the tour Hassle Free, Memorable, Fascinating and Mind Blowing.

overview:
Winter Wanderlust: Embrace the Magic of Frost-Kissed Escapes

Step into a world sculpted by snowflakes and illuminated by the soft glow of winter sunrises—welcome to our Winter Wanderlust header menu at Travelxploria. As a leading Best Travel Agency, we invite you to explore a curated selection of snow-blanketed retreats, ice-rimmed lakes, and festive mountain villages. Whether you’re chasing the glow of the Northern Lights, craving cozy cabin seclusion, or seeking an adrenaline rush on powdery slopes, our itineraries transform frosty dreams into warm memories.

Picture waking to the crisp hush of a winter morning, where steam rises from steaming mugs of cocoa and frost patterns trace delicate lace on windowpanes. Our signature best package options blend heart-pounding activities—like heli-skiing on pristine alpine runs and snowmobiling through silent forests—with moments of serene relaxation. Spend your afternoons wandering lantern-lit lanes at a charming Christmas market, sampling spiced treats baked by local artisans, then retreat to a wood-fired sauna as snowflakes drift outside.

For honeymooners dreaming of an enchanting winter romance, our best honeymoon package offers private chalet stays complete with glass-roofed hot tubs under starlit skies. Imagine toasting champagne as you watch the aurora borealis dance overhead, or sharing a horse-drawn sleigh ride through glittering pines. Our honeymoon specialists partner with boutique inns and luxury resorts to sprinkle each moment with surprise rose petals, personalized love notes, and candlelit dinner arrangements—ensuring your first days as newlyweds feel nothing short of magical.

Families seeking a frosty celebration will delight in our tailored family package experiences. Build snowmen on frozen lake shores, embark on guided snowshoe treks to hidden ice caves, and join friendly huskies for a thrilling dog-sled adventure. Even the littlest explorers can try ice-fishing under expert supervision, while parents unwind beside roaring fireplaces with plush blankets and panoramic mountain views. With Travelxploria as your trusted Best Tour Operator, every safety detail is managed, leaving room for the laughter and joy of winter play.

Crossing hemispheres or continents? Rely on our credentials as the Best International Tour Operator and Best International Travel Agent to handle every winter travel nuance. From arranging seamless transfers to setting up private ski instructors on European slopes, our global network ensures you focus on carving turns, not logistics. Seek the chic après-ski scene of the French Alps, the surreal ice hotels of Scandinavia, or the untouched powder fields of Japan’s northern island—each destination meticulously planned for maximum wonder.

Our Winter Wanderlust theme also celebrates sustainable, low-impact travel. We partner with eco-friendly lodges warmed by renewable energy, support local winter festivals that preserve folk traditions, and offer carbon-offset programs on every booking. These thoughtful choices make Travelxploria the architect of the best value tour package, balancing unforgettable experiences with responsible stewardship of delicate winter ecosystems.

No two travelers share the same vision of a perfect winter escape—so we make personalization our hallmark. Craving a midnight snowshoe trek beneath a full moon? Consider it scheduled. Dreaming of a private ice-sculpting workshop led by a local artisan? We’ll arrange it. Yearning for a gourmet tasting menu featuring truffle-infused fondue amid cozy alpine décor? Your personal culinary concierge awaits. This bespoke approach transforms a standard journey into a deeply personal narrative you’ll recount for years to come.

As you hover over our “Winter Wanderlust” menu, let the promise of crystalline mornings, cinnamon-scented evenings, and exhilarating winter pursuits spark your imagination. With Travelxploria—your Best Travel Agency and unrivaled Best Tour Operator—you’re not simply booking a trip; you’re crafting a snow-dusted chapter of your travel story. Embrace the season’s magic today, and let the world’s most enchanting winter landscapes reveal their wonders.
